name: Best Ever Sponge
recipeInstructions:
This is a favorite never-fail sponge recipe: it does not contain liquid or butter, making it ideal for Swiss rolls. It is simple to adapt to many flavors and will work as a cake or a roll.
1. Have eggs at room temperature.
2. Beat whole eggs in a small bowl with an electric mixer until thick and creamy. The small bowl is necessary to give maximum volume to the eggs; beating time on a moderately high speed should be about 7 minutes.
3. Add sugar, about 1 tablespoon at a time, beating after each addition until sugar is dissolved.
4. While beating eggs and sugar, prepare pans by greasing evenly. Sift dry ingredients together three times to aerate and mix the flours.
5. When sugar is dissolved, transfer mixture to a larger basin for easier folding of ingredients through the egg mixture (it is not necessary to transfer the 2 egg mixture to a larger basin).
6. Sift the flours over the egg mixture using a spatula to lightly fold the flours through. Avoid heavy handling at this stage to prevent a flat, tough sponge.
7. Spread the mixture evenly into pans and bake in a moderate oven for the cooking time specified below:
- 2 eggs: Deep 20cm round cake pan or recess pan about 20 minutes. A deep pan is preferable rather than a sandwich pan as the sponge will be less crusty on top because the high sides protect the top during cooking.
- 3 eggs: Two deep 17cm cake or sandwich pans about 15 minutes, or a 25cm x 30cm Swiss roll pan about 12 minutes.
- 4 eggs: Two deep 20cm round cake pans about 20 minutes, or a deep 23cm round cake pan about 40 minutes.
Variations:
- Citrus: Beat in 2 teaspoons of your favorite grated citrus rind (lemon, mandarin, lime, orange, or grapefruit) with the sugar.
- Nut: Fold in 1/4 cup of any type of ground nuts with the flours.
- Chocolate: Fold in 60g grated dark chocolate with the flours.
Keeping time: 2 days.
